Q1.1:
Welcome!


Study information and consent form 



Thank you for your interest in our study. Overall, this research aims to better understand what influences modern engagement with online news. Specifically, this study explores how different online news content influences people’s willingness to consume it as well as people’s views about such online news content. Before you decide whether to participate, please read the information provided below to understand what the study involves, any potential risks, and your rights as a participant. Your answers are very important to us. We therefore ask you to be attentive and endeavour to answer as accurately as possible.Thank you very much!



Ethics

This study has been reviewed and approved by the University of Bath Ethics Committee (Ethics Reference ID: 10699-12847).



Do I have to take part?

Participation is voluntary, and you can withdraw at any time. Your decision will not affect your relationship with the researchers’ institutions or any other entities.



What would taking part involve?

If you decide to take part, the study will be conducted entirely online and should not take longer than 10 minutes. First, you will be asked to complete some demographic questions. Then, you will be shown a short news article. After reading, we will ask about your interest in receiving a weekly newsletter based on similar articles. After completing the experiment, you will receive a debriefing sheet. All your data will remain anonymous.



What are the benefits of taking part? 

Upon completion, you will receive the basic payment via your Prolific account. Beyond compensation, your participation will contribute to a better understanding of online news consumption, with wider implications for tackling distrust in news sources and supporting a more well-informed society.



What are the risks of taking part?

There are no anticipated risks associated with this study. There should not be any psychological discomfort or embarrassment. However, if you believe you may experience any distress based on the above description, we advise you not to take part. Remember, you may withdraw from the study at any time.



How will my data be processed?

In line with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), your personal data (such as demographic information) will be anonymised and used solely for the purposes of this study. The data will be stored securely, and only the research team will have access to it. All data will be kept confidential, and no identifying information will be included in any reports or publications. Anonymised data may be archived and published for subsequent use in trusted research data repositories.



Who is responsible for compliance with data protection regulations?

The online survey is being conducted by the University of Bath and the WZB Berlin Social Science Center, and is funded by the WZB Berlin Social Science Center. The University of Bath is a public research university in the United Kingdom and complies with the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR). The WZB is a publicly funded research institute and strictly adheres to the legal requirements of the European General Data Protection Regulation (EU GDPR). Personal data will only be processed for scientific research purposes.



If you have any questions about the project, you may contact Dr. Eleonora Alabrese (ea2143@bath.ac.uk) or Prof. Dr. Maja Adena (maja.adena@wzb.eu).



What if something goes wrong?

If you have any concerns or feel distressed during the study, you may withdraw at any point. The research team can be contacted for support at any time.



Your data protection rights

Information on the processing of your data and on data protection, in particular on your right to information, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, objection, data portability and complaints to a supervisory authority, is available on our website (link). You can also send questions about data protection to dataprotection@wzb.eu. You can reach our data protection officer here as well.



Declaration of consent

We ask for your consent for the data processing associated with your participation in the survey. Consent can be revoked at any time with effect for the future (e.g. by contacting the research team). Consent also constitutes the legal basis for data processing.
